title: falsifier_health parallelize Ω-cycle

profile.bottleneck: (a) per-entry python3 fork (~50-150ms x 105) + (b) F12 hexa runtime singleton (4.87s critical-path floor)

strategy_choice.rationale: stdlib-only (bash 3.2 + python3 baseline), clean output ordering by index, JSON-safe parse already in python, single python3 fork per worker (not per entry)

rejected_reasons.OPT-A: GNU parallel not on macOS by default
rejected_reasons.OPT-B: xargs -P loses ordering, awkward escape for multi-line cmds
rejected_reasons.OPT-C: bash background requires manual wait coordination + tmpfile fan-in

impl.python_worker: embedded heredoc materialised to mktemp .py (ProcessPoolExecutor needs importable module path, not <stdin>)
impl.output_ordering: results stored at futs[fut]=idx then awk-sorted by numeric F# prefix in shell render

equivalence.sentinel_seq: __FALSIFIER_HEALTH__ FAIL total=105 clean=103 hit=2 error=0 tampered=0 duration_ms=19000
equivalence.sentinel_par: __FALSIFIER_HEALTH__ FAIL total=105 clean=103 hit=2 error=0 tampered=0 duration_ms=5330

benchmarks.asymptotic_floor_explanation: F12 single-entry 4.87s sets lower bound; -j > 4 gains nothing because critical path is one heavy hexa runtime call

tier_1_outcomes: health_check_all.sh integrated with FALSIFIER_HEALTH_TOOL env (default = parallel, legacy fallback supported)
3.6x wall-clock speedup on falsifier subcheck; ~29s saved on aggregate health_check_all
raw 73 admissibility: per-entry classification IDENTICAL across 105 entries

future_axes: split F12 hexa runtime cmd into hot/cold paths to lower asymptotic floor
extend OPT-D pattern to bridge_health.sh (16 bridges, ~33s sequential, similarly hexa-runtime-bound)
share single python3 worker pool across falsifier+bridge for combined health_check_all -j budget

non_goals: no auto-commit (main thread batches per /loop convention)
sequential tool retained as canonical fallback (raw 71 report-only + safety)
